query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
Research complete. Here's the summary:

## Key Findings

**Most recent EIA-confirmed SPR level (WCSSTUS1):** **331,191 thousand barrels (~331.2M bbl)** for the week ending **June 19, 2026** — confirmed directly from EIA's dnav database and corroborated by YCharts. A secondary source (Trading Economics) reports a further drop to **~325.7M bbl for the week ending June 26**, described as the lowest since May 1983, though this wasn't confirmable from EIA primary data.

**Weekly trajectory — two distinct phases:**
- **Phase 1 (Dec 2025 – late March 2026): Gradual fill/plateau.** Rose from ~411.9M bbl to a peak/plateau of **~415,442 thousand bbl**, held flat from mid-Feb through March 20.
- **Phase 2 (late March – June 2026): Steep drawdown.** Releases accelerated to **~8–10 million bbl/week** in May–June. Cumulatively **~84 million barrels drawn** from the March 20 peak to June 19.

**Policy context (the Wolf Street "twice in 2026" narrative):**
- Trigger was the **Strait of Hormuz closure** amid an Iran conflict (~Feb 28, 2026).
- Mid-March 2026: Trump administration authorized release of **172 million barrels** (~40% of available stockpile) — largest single-country emergency release in history, part of a coordinated 400M-bbl release by 32 IEA nations.
- WTI peaked **~$115/bbl (April 6)**, fell to **~$80/bbl (June 15)** after a US–Iran MOU to reopen the Strait.
- SPR is now at a **four-decade low**; projected to potentially hit operational minimums as early as **August 2026**. No confirmed refill activity yet as of mid-June.

**Note:** FRED does not host this series under WCSSTUS1 (the lookup failed); EIA's dnav petroleum database is the authoritative source. The June 26 figure should be treated as a secondary-source estimate pending EIA confirmation.

sources.snippet: Commercial crude at 412.1M bbl week ending June 19, 2026, down 6.1M bbl, ~7% below five-year average.

sources.snippet: 331.19M bbl for Wk of Jun 19 2026, down from 340.25M last week, down 17.67% from one year ago.

sources.title: Government Uses Strategic Petroleum Reserve as Cudgel to Beat Down Oil-Price Mania: Worked for Second Time
sources.snippet: ~75M bbl released in ~3 months; SPR at 340M (June 12); WTI fell from ~$115 (Apr 6) to ~$80 (June 15).

sources.title: EIA Report Indicates Massive Draw
sources.snippet: SPR declined ~8.0M bbl to 357.1M for week ending May 29, 2026; combined draw ~90M from peak.
